sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel movement data documented for NES version

special_mechanics.name: Marker Movement
special_mechanics.description: Player controls a diamond-shaped marker that moves along edges of playfield and draws lines (Stix) in unclaimed territory. Movement is controlled via four-direction joystick.

physics_values.line_mechanics: marker draws Stix while holding button; cannot cross or backtrack along current line

special_mechanics.name: Fuse Mechanic
special_mechanics.description: If marker stops while drawing, a fuse burns along the drawn line toward the marker. Reaches marker = loss of life. Fuse disappears when marker moves.

special_mechanics.name: Draw Speed Modes
special_mechanics.description: Two drawing speeds: Slow Draw (orange-red, worth double points) and Fast Draw (blue). Speed affects scoring for captured areas.

tier_notes: Qix is a puzzle game without traditional platformer physics. No documented jump, gravity, or acceleration mechanics. Movement is grid/edge-based rather than physics-based. Tier 3 classification appropriate due to lack of detailed movement documentation and puzzle-game nature.
